title: GRI 305-1: Direct (Scope 1) GHG Emissions

paragraphs.text: The organization reported gross direct (Scope 1) GHG emissions of 3,402.997 metric tons of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e) for the reporting year 2024. These emissions arise from fuel combustion in sources under the organization's operational control, specifically natural gas consumption in facilities and diesel fuel consumption in backup generators and fleet vehicles.

The Scope 1 emission sources comprised natural gas combustion contributing 1,199.7924 tCO₂e and diesel combustion contributing 2,203.2044 tCO₂e. Diesel combustion represented 64.7% of total Scope 1 emissions, reflecting the organization's reliance on backup power generation systems and operational vehicle fleet. Natural gas combustion, primarily from HVAC systems and kitchen facilities in office buildings, accounted for the remaining 35.3% of direct emissions.

The organization calculated Scope 1 emissions using the GHG Protocol Corporate Accounting and Reporting Standard (2015 revision). Emission factors were sourced from Indonesia's Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources (MEMR) 2023 National Emission Factors for stationary combustion. Global Warming Potential (GWP) values were applied per the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) Sixth Assessment Report (AR6) 100-year time horizon: CO₂ = 1, CH₄ = 29.8, N₂O = 273.0. The consolidation approach applied was operational control, whereby the organization accounts for 100% of emissions from operations over which it has operational control, regardless of equity share.

Gases included in the Scope 1 calculation were carbon dioxide (CO₂), methane (CH₄), and nitrous oxide (N₂O). The organization did not report material emissions of hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs), perfluorocarbons (PFCs), sulfur hexafluoride (SF₆), or nitrogen trifluoride (NF₃) during the reporting period. Biogenic CO₂ emissions were not material to the organization's operations and are therefore not separately reported.

Compared to the prior year (2023), Scope 1 emissions increased by 2.36%, rising from 3,324.521 tCO₂e to 3,402.997 tCO₂e. This increase of 78.476 tCO₂e was primarily attributable to expanded backup generator runtime during grid maintenance periods and the addition of three new branch locations with diesel-powered emergency systems. The organization has identified this upward trend as requiring corrective action through energy efficiency improvements and renewable energy integration.

**Note on GRI Standard Update**: GRI 305: Emissions 2016, including Disclosure 305-1, has been superseded by GRI 102: Climate Change 2025. The organization will transition to the updated standard for the 2025 reporting period. The current disclosure remains compliant with GRI 305 requirements applicable to the 2024 reporting year.

footnotes: GRI Content Index: 305-1 | Direct (Scope 1) GHG emissions | Section 4.1
Emission factors sourced from Indonesia Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources (MEMR) 2023 National Emission Factors for stationary combustion.
Global Warming Potential (GWP) values applied per IPCC AR6 (100-year time horizon): CO₂ = 1, CH₄ = 29.8, N₂O = 273.0.
Consolidation approach: Operational control per GHG Protocol Corporate Standard.
Reconciliation verified: 1,199.7924 tCO₂e (natural gas) + 2,203.2044 tCO₂e (diesel) = 3,402.997 tCO₂e (total Scope 1).
GRI 305: Emissions 2016 has been superseded by GRI 102: Climate Change 2025. Transition planned for 2025 reporting period.

advisory_recommendations.finding: Scope 1 emissions increased 2.36% year-over-year, driven by diesel combustion (64.7% of total Scope 1), contrasting with peer banks achieving reductions.
advisory_recommendations.benchmark: DBS Group achieved a 12% year-over-year reduction in total operational emissions (Scope 1+2) through renewable energy procurement (62% RE100 compliance) and green building certifications. BRI reduced energy intensity by 7.1% through the BRILiaN Tower green building project, which achieved 19.49% electricity savings and Gold certification from Green Building Council Indonesia (GBCI).
advisory_recommendations.recommendation: Implement a three-phase diesel reduction program: (1) conduct energy audits across all branches to identify backup generator over-reliance, (2) install solar-plus-battery systems at high-diesel-consumption sites to reduce grid dependency, and (3) pursue green building certification (GBCI Gold or LEED) for headquarters and regional hubs, targeting 15-20% energy savings. Establish a 2025 target to reduce Scope 1 emissions by 8% through these interventions.
advisory_recommendations.reference: BRI's BRILiaN Tower achieved 19.49% electricity savings via GBCI Gold certification. DBS Group's RE100 commitment (62% renewable energy) contributed to 12% YoY emissions reduction.

advisory_recommendations.finding: Diesel combustion represents 64.7% of Scope 1 emissions (2,203.2044 tCO₂e), indicating high reliance on fossil-fuel backup power and fleet vehicles.
advisory_recommendations.benchmark: BRI deployed 118 electric vehicles and 150 electric motorcycles as part of its operational decarbonization strategy. DBS Group installed EV charging stations across office locations to support fleet electrification.
advisory_recommendations.recommendation: Develop a fleet electrification roadmap targeting 30% EV conversion by 2026 for operational vehicles (prioritizing high-mileage urban routes). Install EV charging infrastructure at 10 priority branch locations by Q4 2025. For backup power, pilot hybrid solar-diesel systems at 5 branches with highest generator runtime, targeting 40% diesel displacement per site.
advisory_recommendations.reference: BRI's deployment of 118 EVs and 150 e-motorcycles demonstrates feasibility in Indonesian banking sector. DBS Group's EV charging infrastructure supports operational fleet transition.

advisory_recommendations.finding: The organization lacks a Science-Based Target (SBTi) commitment or net-zero target, placing it behind regional peers in climate ambition.
advisory_recommendations.benchmark: BRI became the first financial institution in Indonesia to submit an SBTi commitment letter, targeting net-zero by 2050. DBS Group committed to net-zero by 2050 with interim 2030 targets. Bank Mandiri set a 2060 net-zero target.
advisory_recommendations.recommendation: Submit a Science-Based Targets initiative (SBTi) commitment letter by Q2 2025, establishing a net-zero by 2050 pathway with interim 2030 targets for Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions. Align targets with SBTi Financial Sector Net-Zero Standard. Engage SBTi-approved consultants to develop a decarbonization roadmap including renewable energy procurement (target 50% by 2030), energy efficiency improvements (target 25% intensity reduction by 2030), and fleet electrification (target 50% by 2030).
advisory_recommendations.reference: BRI's SBTi commitment as first-mover in Indonesian banking sector demonstrates regulatory and stakeholder acceptance. DBS Group's 2050 net-zero commitment with interim 2030 targets provides a peer benchmark for ambition level.
